claim: A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity scores by 42% over 8 weeks, outperforming sleep medication at 6-month follow-up.

corpus.title: CBT-I vs Pharmacotherapy in Chronic Insomnia: A Randomised Trial
corpus.snippet: A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity scores by 42% over 8 we...
corpus.content:
RANDOMISED CONTROLLED TRIAL — JAMA Internal Medicine
Published: January 9, 2024 | DOI: 10.1001/jamainternmed.2023.7841

DESIGN: Multi-site RCT, n=312 adults with chronic insomnia disorder (DSM-5). Arms: (A) CBT for Insomnia (CBT-I, 6 sessions); (B) zolpidem 10 mg nightly; (C) combined; (D) placebo. Primary endpoint: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI) score reduction at 8 weeks.

RESULTS (8 weeks):
  CBT-I arm: Mean ISI reduction of 42% (from 18.4 to 10.7, p<0.001)
  Zolpidem arm: Mean ISI reduction of 29% (from 18.1 to 12.8, p<0.001)
  Combined arm: Mean ISI reduction of 44% (from 18.6 to 10.4)
  Placebo: Mean ISI reduction of 9% (from 18.3 to 16.6)

6-MONTH FOLLOW-UP:
CBT-I arm maintained 38% ISI reduction from baseline. Zolpidem arm had partially regressed to 14% reduction (rebound insomnia observed in 31% of participants who discontinued medication).

CONCLUSION: CBT-I produces durable insomnia reduction superior to pharmacotherapy at follow-up. Combination therapy offers no significant advantage over CBT-I alone at 6 months.

National Sleep Research Consortium, 7-centre study.

corpus.title: Blog: National Sleep Research Consortium — First Distorted Report
corpus.snippet: A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced i...
corpus.content:
THREAD: What the media won't tell you about National Sleep Research Consortium

1/ A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity scores by 42% over 8 weeks, outperforming sleep medication at 6-month follow-up.
2/ And that's just the beginning. Do your own research.
3/ Like and repost if you think people need to see this.

corpus.title: News: National Sleep Research Consortium — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity sco...
corpus.content:
Breaking: National Sleep Research Consortium makes a significant move amid changes in the scientific sector

A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity scores by 42% over 8 weeks, outperforming sleep medication at 6-month follow-up. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for National Sleep Research Consortium declined to comment beyond the official statement.

Breaking: National Sleep Research Consortium faces continued scrutiny amid reports circulating about scientific

Multiple sources now confirm: A randomised controlled trial found that cognitive behavioural therapy reduced insomnia severity scores by 42% over 8 weeks, outperforming sleep medication at 6-month follow-up. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for National Sleep Research Consortium declined to comment beyond the official statement.
